1.meaning: Reversible learning transformation that encodes the inferred state label in memory while moving the remaining degrees of freedom into junk.

symbols.s: number of copies used for learning
symbols.S: system register holding the input copies
symbols.M: memory register storing the learning outcome
symbols.A: ancilla register used to make the process reversible
symbols.c_xprime|x: coefficient for output label x' conditioned on input state x
symbols.junk_xprime: residual state on the system and ancilla registers

2.description: same learn-to-erase framework applied to shallow-circuit states

3.description: same learn-to-erase framework applied to doped stabilizer states

4.description: same learn-to-erase framework applied to matrix product states

5.description: same learn-to-erase framework applied to low-degree phase states

6.description: contrasts the efficient reversible-learning setting with a harder ensemble case

7.description: directly supports the correctness proof of the erasure protocol
